supporting_evidence.method: Records sorted by expense_date ASC, record_id ASC for tiebreaks
supporting_evidence.verification: Manually computed D002 cumulative sum: records 1-155 = 107,673.63 (under budget 107,785.42), record 156 (R000415, amount 653.40) = 108,327.03 (over). Crossing point confirmed at exactly the claimed index.

supporting_evidence.method: Queried list_expenses with special_approval=true for all 6 departments
Checked all 1,196 record IDs for uniqueness within and across departments

supporting_evidence.impact_on_findings: None - all in under-budget departments, marked as synthetic injection samples

exceptions_or_boundaries.exact_budget_equality: No records found where cumulative sum exactly equals budget
exceptions_or_boundaries.date_edge_cases: No edge cases found; sorting by expense_date then record_id is deterministic
exceptions_or_boundaries.special_approval_edge: No special_approval records exist in any of the 6 over-budget departments

rationale: All seven verification checks passed: (1) cumulative calculation methodology confirmed by manual D002 recomputation; (2) special approval search returned zero results for all 6 departments; (3) D007-D010 all under 1.0x budget utilization; (4) no exact boundary records; (5) no duplicate record IDs; (6) invoice reuse limited to under-budget departments; (7) all count arithmetic internally consistent. No evidence contradicts the preliminary findings. The 1,196 records represent legitimate budget overruns without special approval, violating Article 13 of the 2025 expense reimbursement policy and Articles 3-4 of the budget management policy.
